channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Chapman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Intel announces its new Battlemage graphics cards, and they might just be the 1440p budget champions we've been waiting for
item.description: Intel announces its newest GPUs, the Intel Arc B580 and Arc B570, with pricing and specs that might make them fantastic 1440p cards on a budget.
item.dc:creator: John.Loeffler@futurenet.com (John Loeffler), TechRadar

item.title: Beyond Bank CIO: Balancing tradition and innovation in the digital age
item.description: Beyond Bank Australia is one of the largest customer-owned banks in Australia and one of the leading B Corps in the country. That certification measures your company’s social and environmental impact, both of which we’re super proud of, but it’s also deeply rooted in our culture.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Robotic shorts give your legs a boost to walk farther and tire less
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how WalkON enhances walking and jogging with sensors and artificial tendons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Unleashes Sweeping Measures Against China's Semiconductor Sector
item.description: US restricts export of chipmaking equipment and high-bandwidth memory, sparking concerns over supply chain disruptions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cyber Monday shoppers expected to set a record on the year's biggest day for online shopping
item.description: Consumers in the United States are scouring the internet for online deals as they look to take advantage of the post-Thanksgiving shopping marathon with Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Delaware judge reaffirms ruling that invalidated massive Tesla pay package for Elon Musk
item.description: A Delaware judge has reaffirmed her ruling that Tesla must revoke Elon Musk’s multibillion-dollar pay package.
item.dc:creator: Randall Chase, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How AI is accelerating—and devaluing—book publishing
item.description: AI has the power to pump out words in record speed. And already, that’s substantially inflating the book publishing market. Lovers of LLMs are pushing into the traditional publishing system, building their own book-publishing entities.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Data broker blunder: More than 600,000 sensitive files exposed in data services leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how 600,000-plus records, including checks and property data, were left exposed online by an information service provider.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 surprisingly practical ways to use ChatGPT’s free voice wizard
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. ChatGPT’s Advanced Voice Mode—free as of Oct 21—is like having a 24/7 wizard in your pocket.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: AWS Updates Amazon Connect to Enhance Customer Experience
item.description: AWS updates Amazon Connect with proactive customer outreach and AI-powered self-service experiences
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bitcoin flirts with $100K as crypto industry rallies for Trump
item.description: Bitcoin has blasted through a series of record highs since President-elect Trump’s election victory as the crypto industry anticipates a significant shift in federal policy.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: Thousands of Amazon workers around the world went on strike Friday, demanding more pay and better working conditions during one of the retail giant's busiest weekends of the year.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Krugman: Hostility to immigrants will hurt America’s tech sector
item.description: If Trump follows through on anti-immigration pledges, the sweeps likely won't spare legal, highly educated workers.
item.dc:creator: Paul Krugman, The Mercury News

item.title: My spur-of-the-moment Chromebook surprise
item.description: If you’ve read this column for long, you probably know that when it comes to tech purchases — and tech decisions in general, really — I’m typically not one to be hasty. It’s practically in my blood at this point.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 10+ tips and services for new Bluesky users
item.description: For the past two or three weeks, upstart social network Bluesky has been attracting large numbers of new users every day, a crowd that quite evidently includes many Apple device users — the app has at times topped Apple’s App Store charts in the run-up to Thanksgiving in the US.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI PCs for federal: The future of edge computing
item.description: How AI PCs supplemented by extreme search capabilities will enable federal employees at the edge to leverage more data to make better decisions, faster. The post AI PCs for federal: The future of edge computing first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco, NTT Data partner to simplify private 5G connectivity
item.description: Cisco and NTT Data are extending their partnership to offer customers more streamlined options for deploying private 5G services for enterprise connectivity. The partnership ties Cisco’s networking technologies to NTT Data’s Transatel network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OPM tries a shift-left approach to anticipating the costs of new tech projects
item.description: When thinking of new technology projects, OPM officials are trying to get a handler on potential costs. The post OPM tries a shift-left approach to anticipating the costs of new tech projects first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
